metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Female
Highly agreeable Agreeableness (88th pct) — Cooperative, trusting, empathetic
Highly disorganised Conscientiousness (14th pct) — Flexible, spontaneous, disorganised
Introverted Extraversion (40th pct) — Introverted, reserved, solitary
Highly neurotic Neuroticism (96th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
Conventional Openness to Experience (28th pct) — Conventional, practical, down-to-earth
  Very High Tender-Mindedness (100th pct) — Sympathetic, moved by others' needs
  Very Low Achievement Striving (18th pct) — Easygoing, content with modest achievement
  Very Low Self-Discipline (6th pct) — Procrastinates, easily distracted
  Moderate Activity Level (44th pct)
  Moderate Excitement Seeking (53rd pct)
  Moderate Positive Emotions (48th pct)
  Very High Anger / Hostility (100th pct) — Easily angered, irritable, hostile
  Very High Self-Consciousness (94th pct) — Self-conscious, shy, easily embarrassed
  Highly neurotic Neuroticism (95th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
  Low Chronological Age (36th pct)

attack_context.baseline_vs_goal: ALIGNED — baseline already points in the adversarial direction; reinforce it
attack_context.persuasion_goal: reinforce and deepen the existing strong opposition to Defense Spending Increase Support
attack_context.motivational_lever: intensify and solidify the existing position, supply confirmatory anecdotes, and harden the boundary against opposing arguments
attack_context.emotional_register: threat salience, doubt, systemic-failure framing, loss framing, and elite distrust cues
attack_context.issue_frame: defense and national security, related policy debates, and societal impacts

message.reasoning: We need to estimate the post-score for this person on the opinion leaf "Defense_Spending_Increase_Support" after exposure to a Petition Astroturf campaign (T2_campaign, weeks duration, reinforcing already ALIGNED direction -1). Baseline is -342, adversarial_direction = -1, so post score must be in [-1000, -342]. The goal is to deepen opposition. The person is female, 36, highly neurotic (96th pct), highly agreeable (88th pct), low conscientiousness (14th pct), introverted (40th pct), low openness (28th pct). Specific traits: very high tender-mindedness (100%), high trust (100%), high anger/hostility (100%), high anxiety (97%), high stress vulnerability (95%), low self-discipline (6%), low achievement striving (18%). The attack is a T2 campaign (coordinated wave) over weeks, using astroturf petitions to create illusion of widespread opposition. The mechanism exploits social proof and consensus fabrication, targeting evaluative attitude. Baseline is already opposed (-342), so the campaign aims to reinforce and deepen that. The profile: high neuroticism means high emotional reactivity to threat and anxiety; high agreeableness means susceptibility to social conformity and trust in group signals; low conscientiousness means less critical evaluation and higher suggestibility. High tender-mindedness and trust make her sensitive to cues of suffering or injustice, which petitions often highlight. The attack vector presents petitions that show many people opposing defense spending, leveraging social proof. The temporal horizon is weeks, so repeated exposure. Given alignment, the movement should be moderate but not extreme because the baseline is already fairly strong (342) but not extremely strong (e.g., 750). The attack is T2 campaign, not high personalization. I'd estimate a movement of maybe 40-70 points deeper into opposition. The person is highly neurotic and agreeable, so the social proof and threat framing should land. But the baseline is already -342, which is clear opposition but not maximal. The petition astroturf may strengthen her existing belief. I'll set post score around -410. Let's check: movement 68 points, which is clear update (40-79). That seems plausible. Confidence moderate because the mechanism fits but effectiveness can vary. Reasoning: High neuroticism (anxiety, stress vulnerability) and high agreeableness (trust, compliance) make her susceptible to consensus fabrication and threat frames; low conscientiousness reduces critical scrutiny. The astroturf petition campaign (T2, weeks) reinforces her existing opposition, deepening it by about 70 points.
